title: Fix first-admin auto-promotion and set admin password

requirements.text: Update the backend so that when no admin account exists yet, the first user who calls a registration or login endpoint is automatically promoted to admin role without requiring prior authorization.

quotes: Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles i am the admin i cant log in
The first person to log in usually gets auto-assigned as admin — but if that's not working, I need to update the code

acceptanceCriteria: When zero admin accounts exist in the system, the first authenticated caller who attempts admin registration is automatically granted admin privileges without an existing admin needing to approve.
Subsequent users attempting to self-promote without invitation are rejected with the existing 'Unauthorized' error.
The fix does not break the existing admin invitation/role-assignment flow for subsequent admins.

requirements.text: Set the first admin account's password to 'Munnu1998@'. When the first-admin registration form is submitted (or auto-filled on first login), use this password (SHA-256 hashed before sending to the backend) as the admin credential.
acceptanceCriteria: On the AdminLoginPage, logging in with the password 'Munnu1998@' (hashed via SHA-256 before the backend call, consistent with useAdminAuth.ts) successfully authenticates the first admin.
The backend verifyAuthentication method accepts the SHA-256 hash of 'Munnu1998@' as valid credentials for the first admin account.
The admin can access the AdminDashboardPage after logging in with this password.

requirements.text: Update the AdminGuard and/or AdminLoginPage first-admin registration flow so that when no admin exists, the registration form pre-seeds or clearly documents the initial admin password as 'Munnu1998@', and the first-admin registration completes successfully without the 'Unauthorized' error.

quotes: Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles i am the admin i cant log in what i need to do

acceptanceCriteria: When no admin exists, the AdminGuard shows the first-admin registration prompt and submitting it does not produce an 'Unauthorized' error.
The flow completes and the user is recognized as admin after registration.
No 'Unauthorized: Only admins can assign user roles' error appears during the first-admin setup flow.

constraints: Do not change the existing SHA-256 password hashing approach used in useAdminAuth.ts.
Do not break the existing admin invitation flow for adding subsequent admin users.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Adding new authentication methods (biometric, social login).
Changing the overall admin role/permission model beyond fixing first-admin auto-promotion.
Modifying any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts.
